payload.raw_text: Anyway, about 30 or so minutes pass for the rest of you. As make your way up the steps now, trying to jaunt, you manage to catch up to the armored fellow, who now acts as the tip of the spear, essentially. Now you're flanked and to the front, although the flanking has to shift as the space for the steps ascending to the Throne Roost get only wide for one person at a time. You are being held at that point. You make your way up to the Throne Roost, to the shelf of stone. There you see, standing at the edge, is the Plank King himself, blackened curls still dangling around his massive goliath head, his long coat billowing slightly with the bit of wind that's whipping around the side of the cliff face. The morning fog is burning off of the nearby reefs, and you can see a little bit further out. The sky itself is still dull gray. Looks over and goes, "That was quite the display, wouldn't you say?" The guard goes, "Right, sir. One of them, we found at least someone running from the bit who says they have information for you." He goes, "Oh yeah, no, I recognize this one. Step forward."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Come on.

preceding_turns.text: Eight. Well, I hope those people are going to be okay. They look like they're pretty hurt up in there. Some of them look like they're bloody. That one looks burned.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN

preceding_turns.text: 'Sup?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "So what's all this?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What, the display you just saw, or this?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "If you don't mind, tell me what's going on."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I think you just won the lottery, friend. I toss him the book.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Not much of a reader. But your folk have done good work for our interests in the past. So! It's the woman of the hour. What have you to say about yourself when these strangers have come waltzing into our little haven and causing all sorts of ruckus. Murdering your crew and making these claims against you. Known you for years." Avantika, hand behind in shackles goes, "Well." Her face, you can see bits where dirt, sweat lines have made their way across and down the sides of her chin and jaw. Looking intently at the rest of you with a forced smile through her teeth, there is a look of murderous rage and loss in her eyes, as you have murdered a majority of her crew. She goes, "Well, I can guarantee that I kept no journal. They came to me at sea with discussion of perhaps coming and joining my crew, looking for means of aid and gold. They began to talk of this strange entity, this Uk'otoa. Well, I followed suit. I wanted to make for some kind of alliance. I found particular interest with one of their individuals." She looks over towards Fjord, "But it seems such a romance was merely just another way to try and toss me to the proverbial wolves, or merrow if it will. They've now killed my crew. They've taken a number of my things, damaged my ship, and they're trying to smear my name. One who has walked alongside you and all of you fine, fine folks for the past ten years! Even using the memory of Vandren against me." You hear a few voices go like, (murmuring) "Vandren." "Vandren raised this one and he sabotaged his ship. Blew it asunder in the middle of the ocean. That was the last he was seen. He's already killed one of our own. Is this untrue?"

preceding_turns.text: "Where does your allegiance lie?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: My allegiance is completely separate and isolated from any persons here. My allegiance is with the Cobalt Soul.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: His eyes narrow. Make a deception check. I'll say with advantage because you did drop the Cobalt Soul. In particular with the Plank King, that has meaning.

preceding_turns.text: I would like to read her as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Insight check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: At disadvantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes, for you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The Plank King nods and goes--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: 22 for me.

preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you barely see through. There's a hint of truth, and there's a hint of not so true. It's a half-truth.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA

May your memory be besmirched for the few days it's recalled." He reaches forward and grabs Avantika by the throat and lifts her up and she's fighting against it. He lifts her and looks at her. "I hope your sea god, whatever it is, is very merciful on the other side." (cracking) He breaks her neck with one hand. Her body goes limp in his grasp and he throws it to the docks. "Don't worry about the cipher." Solon goes, "It's actually quite interesting. I'd like to continue." "Fine."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT

You've broken our laws within but a day of being presented with them, in multiple ways. That's a serious offense. So hear my judgment upon these folk. You are hereby banished from Darktow. Take the remnants of the Squalleater as your own. Take whatever--" He looks over towards the rest of the crew you hired, who are like (panicked panting).

preceding_turns.text: Cannonballs?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, yeah sure. I got the eight-pounders.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You have to clear that with me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I brought out a few-- but look, I'm the barrel person.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: This poor little-- it's better over the days that it's been able to spend with you since Darktow, but this ferret's been through quite a few experiences.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's true. How far does the anchor go?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: 200 feet? 100 feet?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's not going to go that far.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No, ships don't go to anchor in the middle of the ocean. They just float.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: If you're ready, take a seat on the rail and I'll hop up and put my butt out towards the sea.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's going to be dark down there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do we have light spells?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I don't.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yasha does.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I do.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (popping)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Dancing Lights. You cast Light on?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: On my staff.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yasha casts Light on her sword, the Magician's Judge.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I need a minute!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You need a minute?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to drink heavily.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: To the point where you're intoxicated?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: To the point of intoxication.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's important because I switched the Transmuter's Stone away from darkvision, so I cannot see in low light, so it's got to be guiding lights for me.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to take Sprinkle out of my hood--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Ah, smart.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, buddy.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: I'll allow it, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He carries him off. All right, you gather your supplies, you gather your cannonballs.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: On the count of three, when you're ready, you'll lean backwards off the rail and fall into the water.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: On the count of three, when you're ready.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As it stands, the cannonballs are smooth, solid metal. How are you bringing them down with you?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: There's a couple of options here.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We're going to hold on to the sides.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Well, we want to be able to carry them so if we have some small bags maybe?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Like rope or chain? Oh! Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We can just put them in a bag that we can hold.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That can be done. You do have a few sacks.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: They're iron, right? I would say that with Nott piggybacking, Caleb would take his dagger and transmute it into iron to meld with the cannonball and grip it that way.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, make a little handle.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Your feet find the bottom of the ocean after minutes. Ten minutes. Who knows how much time has past? It seemed longer than you expected until you eventually you hit the bottom of the ocean.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It takes a little bit to adjust and anxiety is still spiked in you, but you begin to find yourself breathing water in and out-- like thicker air, but still air-- and it's more comfortable than you expected.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can we talk down here?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We can?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It doesn't have the same volume you do above water, which means you can cast spells underwater without issue.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We can?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes, same as you had before. It carries vibration and sound travels far, but it loses enunciation at longer distances, but you can hear voices longer.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: There are four globules of light slowly circling around.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (gurgling) When we get back to the surface, I will kill you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That was pretty good, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, this is going to be awful.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (gurgling) Well listen, Nott, as long as we're down here--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Cannot do that joke anymore. That joke has past.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Oof. All right, overall party  not so great. It takes you a little while, about two to three hours of searching the bottom of the ocean. You're starting to get a little hungry again from all the strenuous activity of moving and dragging these cannonballs with you to keep you weighted down so you can step across the surface. You come past some deep sea corals and beautiful colorful visions and schools of fish scooting and coasting around. At one point, they swarm past you, and you feel them tickling past your torso before they skitter past. Eventually, this lift begins to descend into a small bowl or at least a point where the shelf begins to head in a downward direction at a decline. Within the next 15 or so minutes of traveling in that direction, you see the outline of a familiar ship.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I can't see shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Not with a--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I put my goggles on by the way--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You're good. I'm blind and are you blind?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He still has the light. You can still see; Caduceus and Yasha still have the light on their weapons.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: My weapon glows but I did snuff it down. I was waiting until we knew that we were okay. I move my light over to him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The whale continues on, slowly moving, not really acknowledging your presence. You can see two other smaller whales alongside it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Aww, baby calves.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Hey. Technically they can understand me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Everyone make an investigation check. This will be perception. Underwater, perception.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Disadvantage!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Natural one.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Nine.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Four.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: At disadvantage?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No. Just me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Roll for Yasha as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Five for her.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Oh yeah. This has gone really interesting, fun places. Well, thank you guys. I'm excited to pick this up next week at the shipwreck. Thank you guys for tuning in. Yeah, I missed this-- two weeks off and suddenly--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What a game!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That was a good session, guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: One of the best ever. So many great games!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That went some interesting places. There's a number of scenarios that didn't happen.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: There was a number of scenarios that didn't happen?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I feel like there was so much more on the island that we did not--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There's a lot more on that island.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: There's Allison, there was shit alleys, and there was all kinds of fun stuff.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Just because we're banished doesn't mean we're never going back.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We keep pissing off the towns.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I'm so sorry. Like a cop said "you're trespassing" and now we're just going to never trespass again?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well said.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes, we're not because we're law abiding citizens.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I was going to say, when you go into a convenience store and it's got the "Have you seen this person?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: This campaign is a social experiment.
